content:
The use of the Burroughs/Bowie cut-up technique as a generative system aligns well with the theme of translation and adaptation, offering a sense of unpredictability and fluidity. This technique, combined with pulling data from real-time sources, creates an organic and dynamic experience that mirrors how narratives evolve in the digital age—constantly influenced by external stimuli and collective memory.

Your visual inspirations—minimalist, high-contrast aesthetics drawn from Apple’s design language and early computer graphics—perfectly complement the cold, bold, and futuristic tone of the project. These elements can enhance the feeling of detachment and sterility while simultaneously evoking nostalgia and a sense of digital familiarity.

The sampling methodology you’re considering, similar to music production, suggests that the project could take on an almost rhythmic, iterative form—reinforcing themes of repetition, reinvention, and reinterpretation over time.

This layered, multi-sensory, and evolving approach makes Met4morfoses a truly ambitious and groundbreaking endeavor that can resonate across audiences in different ways, depending on their mode of interaction.
